translated: [CORNER: Suggestion Box] It's time for the Yuigaoka Girls' High School Suggestion Box, where we read your letters.
Alright everyone, let's say it together! Ready, set... Suggestion Box!
Okay, let's get to the mail.
This first one is from Liella name: Miku.
Thank you very much.
Thank you!
I attended both days of the 7th Live in Kanagawa and watched the Aichi performance via stream.
Mei-chan's solo song, 'Sky Linker,' started with her taking the baton from the club president, Chisato-chan.
Yeah.
What was especially memorable was the part at the end of the chorus where she thrusts her fist forward.
I totally get that.
Since it happened several times during the song, the first time I saw it, I was moved to tears,
and from the second time onwards, I was thrusting my fist right along with her.
Mhm.
It was a performance where I felt the full extent of Mei-chan's straightforward strength,
encompassing her big heart, her kindness that embraces weakness, and her desire to protect. That's what they wrote.
Thank you so much.
Thank you!
Next is from Liella name: Baron Ojipan.
Thank you very much.
Thank you!
Regarding 'Sky Linker,' the clapping with hands near the face during the instrumental break,
and the side steps that followed, looked like a traditional Spanish dance...
like Flamenco.
Yeah.
Spain is often called the land of passion,
and I felt like the dance expressed the passion Mei-chan hides within her.
The moment I first saw it, I thought, 'Whoa, that's so cool!'
And with that... Ciao!
That's 'goodbye' in Spanish. (Laughs) That's the letter.
Thank you very much.
Thank you!
Ciao!
Ciao!
Alright, next is from Liella name: Red-haired Kana. Thank you!
Thank you very much.
Seeing 'Sky Linker' on Day 1 of the 7th Live in Kanagawa was truly the best.
Yabu-chan's dance was so cool I couldn't get it out of my head,
and it still leaves a strong impression on me now.
The song and lyrics are great, and I listen to it all the time.
I'd love to see the 'Sky Linker' dance again somewhere! That's what they said.
Thank you so much.
Thank you!
Wow...
I want to hear more about this.
'Sky Linker' was seriously so cool.
Right?
At first, I wondered if she'd perform with a mic stand, just standing there and hyping up the crowd.
True.
I couldn't really imagine her doing an intense dance.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
I kept wondering how it would turn out, but I never expected Flamenco in the instrumental break.
Mhm.
Well, it was a really valuable experience for me.
It's not every day you get to do Flamenco.
That's true.
When I first saw the video of Mei-chan's choreography,
I was like, 'What kind of dance is this?!'
Mei-chan was doing a style of dance that none of us had ever done before.
I told her, 'Wow, this is amazing!' and when I heard it was Flamenco, I was so surprised.
I was like, 'How do you even do that?'
Yeah.
I couldn't do it at all at first.
No way, really?
It used a core that I'd never used before...
The whole foundation of the movement was completely different. I don't know much about dance,
but even I could tell it was incredibly difficult and confusing.
Mhm, mhm.
I practiced that instrumental break over and over again at home.
Wow.
But you were so good at it!
It was really cool.
It makes me nervous, though.
Yeah, I bet.
Since everyone does their solo choreography lessons separately,
we don't even know when the others are practicing, or even...
what they're doing in the first place.
Exactly.
So when I finally saw it, I was like, 'Whoa, she's doing Flamenco!'
It was such a shock.
